title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Ownership, Access Points, and Public Access · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible least-privilege S3 authorization proof with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-privilege S3 authorization proof and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Principal Action Resource Condition
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 6907 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

Inspect the Principal Action Resource Condition trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Principal Action Resource Condition.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Policy Simulation Negative Tests
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-policy-simulation-negative-tests-ktikv5" that uses case 4375 to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
